micro_segments.title: Introduction to the Scenario and Core Questions

Framework: Nighttime Immersions (Yoledet Status) and Daytime Immersions (Zavah Status) Across Three Weeks

macro_segments.title: Beit Shammai's Calculation: Eighty Nights Plus Daytime Immersions Yielding Ninety-Five

Calculation of Eighty Nights and Fourteen Daytime Immersions Yielding Ninety-Four
The Additional Immersion at the Night of Arrival: Reaching Ninety-Five Total

macro_segments.title: Beit Hillel's Alternative: Counting Only Until the Woman Can Be with Her Husband (Thirty-Five Total)

micro_segments.title: The Sevenfold Structure of Beit Hillel: Seven Nights, Fourteen Days, Seven Days
The Fifth Week as the Cutoff Point: Why Beit Hillel Stops Counting After the First Permissible Encounter

macro_segments.title: The Scope Limitation: Why Thirteen Weeks Rather Than Eighty Days

micro_segments.title: The Halakhic Boundary at Day Eighty: Why the Case Becomes Uninteresting Beyond That Point
Structural Reasoning: Avoiding Half-Weeks and Maintaining Symmetry in the Hypothetical

macro_segments.title: Clarification on Counting: Distinctions Between Yoledet and Zavah, and What Counts as a New Category

micro_segments.title: The Dispute Over Whether Zavah Immersions Count: Yoledet B'Zove (Combined Status)
Why Singleton Days Are Excluded and Only Full Weeks Are Counted
The Shomeret Yom Kineged Yom Exception: Potential Additional Immersions Not Counted

macro_segments.title: Three Lessons Derived from the Entire Analysis

micro_segments.title: First Lesson: Rabbi Akiva's Principle of Tefurim Befanenu (Awareness of Clean Days)
Second Lesson: Rabbi Shimon's Concern About Retroactive Contamination on Day Seven
Third Lesson: Tevilah Bizmanah Mitzvah (The Mitzvah to Immerse at the Proper Time)
Rabbi Yose b'Rabbi Yehuda's Dissent: Going Only When Practically Necessary

macro_segments.title: The Minimum Halachic Threshold for Fetal Viability: Day Forty-One

micro_segments.title: The Mishna's Standard: Before Day Forty-One, No Status as a Fetus; Day Forty-One Onward, Full Status
The Practical Consequence: Uncertainty About Gender and Whether a Miscarriage Even Occurred
Rabbi Yishmael's Position: Different Development Thresholds for Males (Day 41) and Females (Day 81)
The Sages' Rebuttal: Both Male and Female Reach Halachic Viability at Day Forty-One Equally

macro_segments.title: The Cleopatra Precedent: Examining Empirical Evidence for Fetal Development

micro_segments.title: The Historical Account: Cleopatra's Dissection of Pregnant Maidservants to Determine Development
Rabbi Yishmael's Appeal to Scripture Over Empirical Evidence
The Sages' Critique: Methodological Flaws in the Cleopatra Experiment (Lack of Control, Uncertain Conception Date)
The Principle of Ein Apotropus L'Arayot: Why Sexual Oversight Cannot Be Guaranteed
Abaye's Resolution: Even at Equal Development Stages, Different Maturation Rates Are Possible

macro_segments.title: The Halakhic Consensus: Day Forty-One for Both Genders, Despite Ambivalence Toward Empirical Proof

micro_segments.title: The Default Rule Despite Rabbi Yishmael's Textual Reasoning: Majority Opinion Controls

macro_segments.title: Aggadic Discourse: The Fetus in the Womb—Physical Development and Spiritual Elevation

micro_segments.title: Rabbi Simlai's Description: The Folded Posture and Closed Mouth, Open Navel

Birth as Transformation: The Opening of What Was Closed and Closing of What Was Open
The Candle Over the Head: Universal Vision and Complete Knowledge of Torah in Utero
The Perfection of the Womb Versus the Imperfection of the World: The Smack and Forgetting

topical_tags.term: tevilah bizmanah mitzvah (immersion at the proper time as a mitzvah)
Beit Shammai vs. Beit Hillel (dispute on counting immersions)
yoledes (status of a woman after childbirth)
zavah (woman with irregular bleeding)
tevul yom aroch (extended purification status)
niddah (menstruating woman)
tefurim befanenu (clean days must be witnessed/observed)
shomeret yom kineged yom (woman watching one day opposite one day)
Torah's enumeration of tumah and taharah days (forty and eighty)
fetal viability threshold (day 40 vs. day 41)
Rabbi Yishmael's position on male (41 days) vs. female (81 days) development
Cleopatra dissection experiment (empirical evidence for fetal development)
ein apotropus l'arayot (no guardianship over sexual matters)
Rabbi Akiva (requires awareness for clean days to count)
Rabbi Shimon (concern about retroactive contamination on day seven)
Rabbi Yose b'Rabbi Yehuda (immerse only when practically needed)
fetal posture and development (folded, closed mouth, open navel)
Torah knowledge in utero (melamdin oto kol Torah)
the angel's smack at birth (forgetting of Torah)
safek (doubt/uncertainty)
